metadata.citation: Chetty, Friedman, Hendren, Jones, Porter (2018). The Opportunity Atlas: Mapping the Childhood Roots of Social Mobility.
metadata.geography: All U.S. Core-Based Statistical Areas (metropolitan + micropolitan)
metadata.aggregation_method: Population-weighted average of tract-level kfr_pooled_pooled_p25, weighted by pooled_pooled_count (number of children in tract cohort). Tracts mapped to counties via first 5 digits of tract FIPS code, counties mapped to CBSAs via 2020 Census delineation crosswalk.

key_metric.definition: Mean household income rank in adulthood for children born to families at the 25th income percentile. Higher = better upward mobility. Theoretical range 1-100, typical range 35-55.
